name: Salas, Cascada de Nonaya, La Espina – ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around Salas Asturias offers a diverse landscape, characterized by its mountainous and valley terrains bordering the Cantabrian mountain range. The region features demanding climbs and descents, with trails traversing dense forests, open tracks, and unpaved sections. This topography provides a varied environment for mountain bikers, from technical routes to more accessible paths. Best mountain bike trails around Salas The most popular mountain bike trail is Pravia, Playa el Garruncho, La Quinta de Selgas en El Pitu, Cudillero – ruta circular, a 32.3 miles (52.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route connects coastal areas with cultural sites. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Salas, Cascada de Nonaya, La Espina – ruta circular, a moderate 14.2 miles (22.8 km) path. It features natural landmarks like the Cascada de Nonaya waterfall. Local mountain bikers also love the Ponte de Galiñeiro – Salas Historic Town Center loop from Grado, a 56.4 miles (90.8 km) trail leading through the historic center of Salas, often completed in about 7 hours 24 minutes. Mountain biking around Salas is defined by rugged mountainous terrain, lush valleys, and dense forests, with trails that range from challenging technical sections to smoother forest tracks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Salas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Salas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Salas?
A: Salas offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with nearly 40 routes available for exploration. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult, ensuring there's something for every mountain biker.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Salas?
A: The terrain around Salas is diverse, characterized by its mountainous and valley landscapes bordering the Cantabrian mountain range. You can expect demanding climbs and descents, with trails traversing dense forests, open tracks, and unpaved sections. Some routes feature technical sections with rocks, ruts, and roots, while others offer smoother forest tracks.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails in Salas?
A: Yes, Salas has 14 moderate mountain bike trails. One popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027482973/salas-cascada-de-nonaya-la-espina-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salas, Cascada de Nonaya, La Espina – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 22.8 km long and takes about 1 hour 57 minutes to complete, featuring natural landmarks like the Nonaya waterfall.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Salas?
A: The mountain bike trails in Salas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging routes and scenic landscapes.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that pass through the historic center of Salas?
A: Yes, you can cycle through the historic center of Salas on a mountain bike. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40318078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte de Galiñeiro – Salas Historic Town Center loop from Grado&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 90.8 km trail that takes approximately 7 hours 24 minutes to complete, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural exploration.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that include coastal views or attractions near Salas?
A: Absolutely. The region's proximity to the coast allows for routes that combine mountain biking with coastal scenery. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027484814/pravia-playa-el-garruncho-la-quinta-de-selgas-en-el-pitu-cudillero-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pravia, Playa el Garruncho, La Quinta de Selgas en El Pitu, Cudillero – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; is a 52 km trail that connects coastal areas with cultural sites, offering diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural monuments accessible by mountain bike in Salas?
A: Yes, you can encounter natural features like waterfalls on some routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027482973/salas-cascada-de-nonaya-la-espina-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salas, Cascada de Nonaya, La Espina – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; specifically features the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/865581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nonaya Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Other natural monuments in the wider area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/805170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock arch at La Caladoira beach&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/93692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of Silence&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What other attractions can I see near the mountain bike trails in Salas?
A: Beyond the trails, Salas and its surroundings offer several cultural and natural attractions. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5125287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of St. Mary Major in Salas&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/363182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Regalina Chapel&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2005855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Concha de Artedo Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also close to natural parks like Somiedo and Las Ubiñas La Mesa.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike loops for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders looking for longer, challenging loops have several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10504446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Degollada Recreation Area – Pico Friera loop from Aces&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 59.9 km route, while a similar difficult loop, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Degollada Recreation Area – Pico Friera loop from Grado&lt;/a&gt;, covers 51.8 km.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Salas, Asturias?
A: Asturias generally offers pleasant conditions for outdoor activities from spring through autumn. While specific conditions vary, the diverse terrain means you can find suitable trails for much of the year. Summer provides warmer weather, while spring and autumn offer milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Winter can bring challenging conditions, especially in higher mountainous areas.

Q: Are there any facilities or points of interest like fountains along the trails?
A: While specific facilities vary by trail, the region does have points of interest such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145617" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de Pain&lt;/a&gt;, which can be found in the vicinity of some routes. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water and supplies, especially on longer or more remote trails.
tips: Very simple fountain, built in stone, with a stone plate in which the spout is located, the water falling into a stone basin.
Beautiful waterfall in the middle of the forest. In summer you can dip your tired feet in the icy water!
Puerta de Occidente and Ruta Jacobea, is one of the best preserved medieval towns in Asturias. Enjoy its medieval fortresses, palatial houses and its Indian architecture.
